content:
Let $R=2008$ and set 
$$r_n=\text{radius of }C_n,\quad a_n=\sqrt{r_n}.$$ 
A little distance‐chasing shows that for $n>1$ the centers of $C_{n-1}$ and $C_n$ must satisfy
$$(a_n-a_{n-1})\,\sqrt R=a_{n}a_{n-1},$$
i.e.
$$\frac1{a_{n-1}}-\frac1{a_n}=\frac1{\sqrt R}\,.$$
Since $r_1=1$, we have $a_1=1$, so by telescoping
$$\frac1{a_n}
=\frac1{a_1}-(n-1)\frac1{\sqrt R}
=1-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt{2008}}\,.$$
Hence
$$a_n>0\iff1-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt{2008}}>0
\iff n-1<\sqrt{2008}
\iff n<\sqrt{2008}+1\approx45.82.$$
The largest integer $n$ is $\boxed{45}$.

Let $R=2008$ and set 
$$r_n=\text{radius of }C_n,\quad a_n=\sqrt{r_n}.$$ 
A little distance‐chasing shows that for $n>1$ the centers of $C_{n-1}$ and $C_n$ must satisfy
$$(a_n-a_{n-1})\,\sqrt R=a_{n}a_{n-1},$$
i.e.
$$\frac1{a_{n-1}}-\frac1{a_n}=\frac1{\sqrt R}\,.$$
Since $r_1=1$, we have $a_1=1$, so by telescoping
$$\frac1{a_n}
=\frac1{a_1}-(n-1)\frac1{\sqrt R}
=1-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt{2008}}\,.$$
Hence
$$a_n>0\iff1-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt{2008}}>0
\iff n-1<\sqrt{2008}
\iff n<\sqrt{2008}+1\approx45.82.$$
The largest integer $n$ is $\boxed{45}$.
